# Triggers


## What a trigger is

A binding that tells iii when to invoke a function HTTP requests, cron schedules, queue messages,
state changes, log events, and stream events are all examples of triggers.

## Trigger types

How trigger types are declared by workers and bound to functions.

## Trigger components

The three parts of a trigger: trigger type, config, and function ID.

## Trigger pipeline

How a registered trigger fires and routes to its function, including the optional condition gate
before the handler runs.

## Trigger lifecycle

The states a trigger moves through: registered, active, invoked, unregistered.

## Multiple triggers per function

One function can be invoked by many triggers, mixing HTTP, queue, cron, and other types.

## Trigger conditions

Optional `condition_function_id` that runs before the handler; the handler only fires if the
condition returns truthy.
